executive-summary.primary-cause: Server-side function invocation failure during image upload

step.description: Successfully created blob URL for processed image in browser memory
Failed to upload processed image to server endpoint

issue.title: Empty Request Body
issue.description: The POST request to /api/images/direct-upload contains no body data (content-length: 0). This indicates the image blob was not properly serialized and sent with the request.

probable-causes.cause: FormData not properly created or populated with blob data
Blob reference lost during async operation
Incorrect fetch() API usage - body parameter missing or empty
CORS or content-type mismatch preventing body transmission

location.context: Direct upload initiation in background removal handler

issue.title: Vercel Serverless Function Failure
issue.description: The Vercel serverless function backing /api/images/direct-upload failed to execute properly. This is a server-side runtime error, not a client-side issue.

probable-causes.cause: Function timeout (default 10s for Hobby tier, 60s for Pro)
Memory limit exceeded during image processing
Missing environment variables or API keys
Unhandled exception in serverless function code
Cold start failure - function initialization error

deployment-context.note: Edge function or Node.js runtime issue

issue.title: Image Processing Pipeline Disconnect
issue.description: The local image processing completed successfully (blob created, gallery updated), but the server-side persistence failed. This creates a state where the user sees the processed image but it's not saved/persisted to the backend.

impact.item: User sees processed image but cannot save project
Processed image lost on page refresh

browser-info.user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/142.0.0.0 Safari/537.36

notable-observations.observation: No Origin header present (potential CORS issue)

intended-flow.step: 1. User triggers background removal on image
2. Client-side processing creates blob with background removed
6. Client updates UI with persistent URL

actual-flow.step: 4. Server function failed to execute (500 error)

primary-hypothesis.title: Request Body Not Properly Serialized
primary-hypothesis.explanation: The most likely root cause is that the client-side code creates the blob correctly but fails to properly package it into the fetch() request. The content-length of 0 indicates the request body is empty, meaning the blob data never made it to the server. This could be due to: - Not awaiting blob.arrayBuffer() or similar async operation - FormData not properly appended with blob - fetch() body parameter set to undefined or null - Race condition where blob is garbage collected before transmission

validation-steps.step: Check paint.js:415 for fetch() body parameter
Verify FormData creation and blob appending
Confirm Content-Type header is set (multipart/form-data or application/octet-stream)
Add logging to verify blob size before transmission

secondary-hypothesis.title: Serverless Function Configuration Issue
secondary-hypothesis.explanation: The Vercel function may be misconfigured or missing required dependencies. Even if the request body was empty, a well-designed API should return a 400 Bad Request, not 500 Internal Server Error. The 500 suggests an unhandled exception or configuration issue.

validation-steps.step: Check Vercel function logs for detailed error message
Confirm all dependencies are included in deployment
Check environment variables are properly set

action.title: Fix Request Body Serialization
action.description: Review paint.js:415 and ensure the blob is properly converted to a format suitable for transmission (FormData with multipart/form-data or raw binary with proper headers).

action.title: Review Serverless Function Implementation
action.description: Check the /api/images/direct-upload endpoint implementation for: - Proper error handling for empty/missing body - Try-catch blocks to prevent unhandled exceptions - Appropriate timeout configuration - Memory limits for image processing

action.title: Add Client-Side Validation
action.description: Validate blob exists and has data before attempting upload. Show clear error messages to user if validation fails.

action.title: Implement Better Error Handling
action.description: Add detailed error logging for both client and server to capture: - Blob size before transmission - Request headers and body structure - Server-side function logs via Vercel dashboard

action.title: Add Health Check Endpoint
action.description: Create a test endpoint to verify the upload pipeline works independently of the background removal feature.

action.title: Implement Fallback Strategy
action.description: If server upload fails, keep blob in memory and retry, or allow user to download processed image directly without server persistence.

verification-checklist.item: Review paint.js:415 - verify fetch() includes body parameter with blob data
Check Vercel function logs for detailed error stack trace
Verify Content-Type header is properly set for binary data
Test with smaller images to rule out size/timeout issues
Add console.log statements before fetch() to verify blob size
Test upload endpoint independently with curl or Postman
Review Vercel deployment logs for function build errors
Check Vercel dashboard for function memory/timeout metrics

additional-notes.note: The TouchEvent passive listener warning is unrelated to this failure but should be addressed separately for better scroll performance.
The gallery successfully updated with the blob URL, indicating client-side processing is working correctly. The issue is purely with server communication/persistence.
Users may experience data loss - processed images are not persisted and will be lost on page refresh. This should be communicated clearly in the UI until fixed.

conclusion.summary: The remove background worker fails at the server upload stage due to an empty request body (content-length: 0) combined with a serverless function invocation failure (500 error). The primary issue is likely in paint.js:415 where the blob is not properly serialized into the fetch request body. Secondary concerns include potential Vercel function configuration issues that cause a 500 error instead of proper error handling.

dependencies.dependency: Access to Vercel function logs for detailed error trace
Code review of paint.js background removal handler
